title: Majthal Sanctuary
tagline: Majthal Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: The Majthal Sanctuary is situated in Solan District and has steep and rugged terrain.
overview.description: The Majthal Sanctuary is situated in Solan District and has steep and rugged terrain. The sanctuary is about 10 km on the kacha road Kararaghat (Shimla-Bilaspur Highway) to Kashlog. The sanctuary is said to have a large population of endangered cheer pheasant, and there is also a large goral population. Recently, a globally threatened bird called the grey-crowned prinia was discovered by Virender Kumar Bhardwaj and Rakeshwar Kapoor.

topPlaces.name: Darlaghat
topPlaces.description: Darlaghat is a village panchayat in Solan district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is famous for its Ambuja Cement plant.

items.note: Darlaghat is a village panchayat in Solan district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. It is famous for its Ambuja Cement plant.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Majthal Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Majthal Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Majthal Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (71 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Majthal Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Majthal Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Majthal Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Majthal Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
